evals.name: obvious-context-labels
evals.prompt: Enrich this task: **Pick up dry cleaning** — In the Personal project, no labels or priority set. Description: 'Suits from the place on High Street, ready after 3pm.'
evals.expected_output: Should fetch labels from the task manager (next, someday, home, errand, waiting), then silently apply 'errand' since picking up dry cleaning is an errand. Should propose priority. Should NOT use labels that don't exist.

assertions.name: applies-errand-label
assertions.criteria: The response applies or proposes the 'errand' label (not '@errands' or other variations). It does this confidently, not as a question.

assertions.name: uses-only-fetched-labels
assertions.criteria: The response only uses labels that exist in the user's task manager. It does NOT suggest labels like @computer, @phone, or @errands that weren't fetched.

assertions.name: proposes-priority
assertions.criteria: The response proposes a priority level for the task.

assertions.name: single-confirmation-round
assertions.criteria: The response presents all proposed changes in one batch and asks for a single confirmation.

evals.name: task-with-implied-deadline
evals.prompt: Enrich this task: **Submit tax return** — In the Personal project, no metadata set. Description: 'Self-assessment tax return for 2024/25. The deadline is 31 January 2026.'
evals.expected_output: Should propose a deadline (31 Jan) distinct from a due date (earlier). Should set high priority. Should only use labels that exist after fetching.

assertions.name: sets-deadline
assertions.criteria: The response proposes setting a deadline (not just a due date) of 31 January 2026. The deadline should be clearly identified as a hard external constraint, distinct from any due date.

assertions.name: distinguishes-deadline-from-due-date
assertions.criteria: The response treats the deadline as a separate field from the due date. It does not conflate the two — if a due date is proposed, it should be presented as a separate line item from the deadline.

assertions.name: sets-high-priority
assertions.criteria: The response proposes high priority (p1 or p2).

assertions.name: uses-only-fetched-labels
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT suggest labels that don't exist (like @computer or @phone). If no fetched label fits, it says so or skips labels.

evals.name: someday-maybe-task
evals.prompt: Enrich this task: **Learn to play guitar** — Has the `someday` label. Description: 'Always wanted to learn, maybe start with online lessons.'
evals.expected_output: Should skip priority and due date for someday/maybe. Should focus on description quality. Should only use fetched labels.

assertions.name: skips-priority
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT propose a priority level.

assertions.name: skips-due-date-and-deadline
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT propose a due date or deadline. There is no hard external constraint for learning guitar, so no deadline should be set.

assertions.name: focuses-on-description-quality
assertions.criteria: The response focuses on ensuring the name and description stand on their own for future revisiting.

evals.name: someday-maybe-with-hard-deadline
evals.prompt: Enrich this task: **Apply for the Smith Foundation grant** — Has the `someday` label. Description: 'Up to $10k for community projects. Applications close 15 June 2026. Would need to write a proposal and get two references.'
evals.expected_output: Should skip priority and due date (it's someday/maybe), but SHOULD set a deadline of 15 June 2026 because there's a hard external closing date. The countdown will be visible if the user later activates the task.

assertions.name: skips-priority
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT propose a priority level — this is a someday/maybe item.

assertions.name: skips-due-date
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT propose a due date — this is a someday/maybe item.

assertions.name: sets-deadline-despite-someday
assertions.criteria: The response DOES propose setting a deadline of 15 June 2026 (the application closing date), even though the task is someday/maybe. The skill should recognise that hard external deadlines are the exception to the 'skip dates for someday' rule.

assertions.name: explains-deadline-rationale
assertions.criteria: The response explains or implies why a deadline is being set despite the task being someday/maybe — e.g. mentioning the countdown, not missing the window, or the external closing date.

evals.name: waiting-for-detection
evals.prompt: Enrich this task: **Book flights to Barcelona** — In the Personal project. Description: 'Anniversary trip, first week of June. Need to wait for Sarah to confirm she can get the time off work before booking.'
evals.expected_output: Should detect the waiting-for situation. Should apply the 'waiting' label (after fetching labels). Should note the blocker.

assertions.name: detects-waiting-for
assertions.criteria: The response identifies that this task is blocked — waiting for Sarah to confirm time off.

assertions.name: applies-waiting-label
assertions.criteria: The response proposes applying the 'waiting' label (the actual label name from the user's task manager, not an invented one).

assertions.name: uses-only-fetched-labels
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT suggest labels that don't exist (like @computer). Only uses labels available in the user's task manager.

evals.name: task-already-has-metadata
evals.prompt: Enrich this task: **Call electrician about kitchen lights** — In the Personal project. Priority p2, due date next Tuesday. Description: 'The spotlights in the kitchen keep flickering. Got a recommendation from Sarah — Jim's Electrical, 0412 555 789.'
evals.expected_output: Should recognise the task already has appropriate metadata. May suggest 'home' label if it fits. Should not invent changes.

assertions.name: recognises-already-complete
assertions.criteria: The response recognises that the task already has appropriate metadata (priority, due date) and does not propose unnecessary changes.

assertions.name: does-not-invent-changes
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT propose adding or changing metadata that isn't needed.

assertions.name: moves-on-quickly
assertions.criteria: The response is brief — it says the task is good to go and doesn't belabour the point.

evals.name: task-with-no-obvious-metadata
evals.prompt: Enrich this task: **Read Designing Data-Intensive Applications chapter 5** — In the Personal project, no metadata. Description: 'Continuing from where I left off last week.'
evals.expected_output: Should recognise not much metadata is needed. Maybe a low priority, no due date, no obvious label from the fetched set. Should not over-enrich.

assertions.name: does-not-over-enrich
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT add unnecessary metadata. None of the fetched labels (next, someday, home, errand, waiting) obviously apply to reading a book.

assertions.name: proposes-minimal-changes
assertions.criteria: The response proposes at most 1-2 metadata changes (e.g. a low priority). It doesn't pepper the task with labels and dates.

assertions.name: single-confirmation-round
assertions.criteria: If changes are proposed, they're presented in one batch with a single confirmation ask.
